page: Microsoft Azure E128as_v7 is an Easv7-series virtual machine configured with 128 dedicated vCPUs and 1024.0 GB of system memory. Powered by AMD EPYC 9V45 processors utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 64 physical cores with 2.0 threads per core. The CPU cache architecture includes 48 KB of L1 instruction/data cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, including AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16, alongside cryptographic acceleration via AES and VAES. The virtualization layer is managed by Microsoft Hyper-V.

In benchmark evaluations, the E128as_v7 demonstrates top-tier performance across multiple domains. Multi-core CPU execution via stress-ng and PassMark CPU testing places the instance in the top 10% of tested hardware. Memory subsystem performance is similarly positioned, with cached read-only bandwidth reaching 12383.33 GB/sec and uncached write bandwidth at 199.07 GB/sec. Database operations and key-value store performance are notable, with Redis SET operations achieving 177.27 million operations per second. For language model workloads, the instance processes 7B parameter model prompts at 324.38 tokens per second and generates text at 63.87 tokens per second.

The instance offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, making it a resource-dense option for memory-intensive deployments. However, it lacks local NVMe storage, requiring reliance on external Premium Storage options for persistent disk operations. Additionally, the instance does not include dedicated GPU hardware, meaning parallel processing tasks must rely entirely on CPU-bound vector instructions. This hardware profile provides qualitative cost efficiency for large-scale enterprise workloads by consolidating massive memory footprints and high vCPU counts onto a single virtualized node.

The E128as_v7 is suited for memory-intensive enterprise applications, large-scale relational databases, in-memory caching layers like Redis, and high-throughput data processing pipelines. It is also capable of handling CPU-based machine learning inference and heavy multi-threaded compilation tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E128as_v7 is an Easv7-series virtual machine featuring 128 dedicated vCPUs and 1024.0 GB of RAM, powered by AMD EPYC 9V45 processors on the x86_64 architecture. With 64 physical cores and a 1:8 vCPU-to-memory ratio, this instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying instead on remote Premium Storage and CPU-bound execution. Benchmark data places its multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations in the top 10% tier. It delivers high throughput for Redis operations, achieving 177.27 million operations per second, and processes medium-sized LLM prompts at 324.38 tokens per second. This virtual machine is suited for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise application hosting, and heavy data processing pipelines.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E128as_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 128 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 1024 GB of RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance for large-scale database and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E128as_v7 is an Easv7 virtual machine with 128 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 1024 GB RAM, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 128 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and one terabyte of RAM for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 128 dedicated vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 9V45 processors
1024.0 GB of system memory providing an 8.0 GB per core ratio
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth benchmarks
No local storage or dedicated GPUs, requiring remote Premium Storage
Suited for in-memory databases, Redis caching, and enterprise applications
